explicit_text: I was born and lived in Mexico City for 40 years. Most of my work family also came from there, and despite its pollution and traffic, it shaped who I am. Leaving Mexico City was hard, but I wanted a better life for my children. I often miss my sister and my mom who still live there.

explicit_text: Mexico is my home country where I was born and raised. The situation there became very harsh with insecurity, a deficient education system, and low salaries, which motivated me to move. Despite these challenges, I believe people in Mexico are honest and hardworking. My roots there remain important to me even after moving.

explicit_text: After my husband passed away, I decided to move to the United States with my children for better opportunities and security. We have been living here for 15 months, adapting to the language and cold winters, especially in Anchorage, Alaska. Visiting the U.S. for the first time was a wonderful experience, including trips to Disneyland and SeaWorld. Living here has fulfilled a childhood dream of mine.

explicit_text: I currently live in Anchorage, Alaska with my children and my new husband Chris. It has been challenging adjusting to the long, cold winters and the language, but it has also brought us closer as a family. Anchorage offers nature and security that I couldn't find in Mexico City. This is where I met my husband through my uncle, and where we are building a new life together.

This visit was part of a larger trip that also included SeaWorld and Legoland, making it very special for us.
